programmes.show_description: How The Rogue Roar'd: When Frank and Lu are hired to help close down an old bingo hall, Frank is surprised to find an old nemesis at work.
The Price of Fame: An involving mystery from the award-winning mind of Reginald Hill. When an entertainer's body is washed up on a beach, Pascoe faces a case without Dalziel.
Jobs for the Girls: Chigwell comedy with the Essex girls. Sharon and Tracey want spending money for Christmas, so they take up some extra work to pay for last minute presents.
Ashes Denote That Fire Was: The greater Copper family, uptown and Five Points alike, gathers around Corcoran, who is reeling from a loss. Freeman steps beyond his role as doctor.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Someone Else's Baby: More laughs with the Essex sisters. Sharon and Tracey decide to search through their mother's belongings for undiscovered treasure.
Cover Stories: Burnside discovers Carver's drinking habit, but he has secrets of his own to protect.
New. And the Winner Is: Julia secretly enters Vivien into a recipe competition but the cautious cook is reluctant to give an interview when she emerges as the winner.
New. Searching: Nick helps a girl in care who refuses to accept that her mother is dead. Meanwhile, Eva tells Jimmi she has asked for a transfer.
Past Caring: Ed admits to Tom that he's being blackmailed, and Ric is unimpressed to find Robert has been readmitted, but feels more sympathetic as he discovers the extent of his injuries.
The Blame Game: Jordan bears the brunt of an attack on the ED from the Press and public, while Ruth eagerly awaits news regarding her application for a surgical job.
Cover Stories: Burnside discovers Carver's drinking habit, but he has secrets of his own to protect.
New. With Bianca and Ricky making their way to the church, Carol's appearance is in danger of ruining the wedding celebrations. Meanwhile, Janine is determined to get revenge.
New. At the Vic, everyone toasts the couple. Becca sneaks upstairs to return Ronnie's hairbrush.
Ace, King, Queen, Jack: Nostalgic fun in the Great British series about vets. A hilarious episode as James is dreading the Darrowby dog show because of Mrs Pumphrey's dog, Tricky Woo.
Tom and Emma tell Jack about their intention to accept Charles' offer, while Polly and Gerald fall out over their business plans.
See Thyself, Devil!: When a washed-up rock star thinks the Devil is trying to kill him, Frank and Lu must uncover the truth before the real killer strikes again.
Shift!: Catch up with Chigwell's finest. Sharon is enjoying her new life in Tracey's home and helping herself to the prawns in the fridge, Tracey is having money troubles.
Who's That Talking to Lenny?: More hijinks with those young-at-heart OAPs. Lenny from the pickle factory hears a voice from above, and waits patiently for more information.
Oh Look! Mitzi's Found Her Mummy: Popular sitcom. Clegg looks after a friendly little dog for the day, but when Truly, Billy and Alvin arrive at his house, the animal turns ferocious.
Plenty of Room in the Back: More silliness with those sprightly OAPs. Auntie Wainwright admits that she needs a new means of transport and sends Tom and Smiler to collect it.
Sailing Close To The Wind Part 1: After a revered member of the sailing club is brutally bludgeoned to death, Judith, Becks and Suzie investigate.
Sailing Close To The Wind Part 2: Motives stack up against the suspects, Kit's killer sends another violent warning and Judith, Becks and Suzie are in a race against time.
Another adventure for the eccentric ex-detectives. The team re-opens the unsolved abduction of two 10-year-old boys. Gerry struggles to give up smoking.
See Thyself, Devil!: When a washed-up rock star thinks the Devil is trying to kill him, Frank and Lu must uncover the truth before the real killer strikes again.
Well-Schooled in Murder: Lynley and Havers respond to a call for help from a friend working at a prestigious public school to investigate the disappearance of a promising pupil.
Someone Else's Baby: More laughs with the Essex sisters. Sharon and Tracey decide to search through their mother's belongings for undiscovered treasure.
Think Gently of the Erring: Corcoran is staggered to discover someone close to him in chains. Dr Matthew Freeman approaches Robert Morehouse to suggest a joint effort.
Shopping from Home.
Shopping from Home.
Keeping Up Appearances: No, not Mrs Bucket - Sharon and Tracey! Tracey is shocked to find that two years in prison have finally taken their toll on Darryl.
Security: While Quinnan tries to catch the person who is threatening his friend, finding the right moment to propose becomes increasingly difficult. More grief for Polly!
New. A Pain in The....: Julia's friend makes her an enticing business offer with a cryptic suggestion, while Archie deals with a traffic warden suffering from sciatica.
New. Shine a Light On: Jimmi learns he is not cut out for public speaking when nerves get the better of him as he gives a lecture on forensics.
When Lightning Strikes Twice: Tom goes behind Zubin's back to undertake a new procedure on a patient with severe angina, and Rosie is forced to confront her true feelings for Mubbs.
Chaos Theory: Jordan rallies the team after introducing an unpopular new policy, and a boy is suspected of taking and selling LSD when he is admitted after falling from a school building.
Security: While Quinnan tries to catch the person who is threatening his friend, finding the right moment to propose becomes increasingly difficult. More grief for Polly!
New. A live episode of EastEnders in which the Walford residents enjoy Ricky and Bianca's wedding reception and the identity of Archie's killer is revealed.
New. While the Square comes to terms with recent events, an irate Zainab gets stuck in the unit and her waters break. Meanwhile, Ian and Jane discover Lucy alone with Leon.
The Healing Touch: A trip through time to more gentle days with the Yorkshire vets, one of whom went on to play a famous Time Lord. The vets visit Granville Bennett's animal hospital.
Sarah is caught trying to steal a crucial piece of evidence from Ken's office, and Emma tells Jan about Tom's true feelings.
The Sticking Place: Long-buried secrets are unearthed when Frank and Lu come to the aid of a community of allotment holders threatened with eviction.
Women's Troubles: More laughs from the two cockney sparrows, Sharon and Tracey. Whilst Sharon is out enjoying herself with her new fella, Tracey is convinced she is pregnant.
The Second Stag Night of Doggy Wilkinson: The world's longest-running sitcom! Absent-minded Doggy is disappointed that his stag night isn't as exciting as his first.
What Happened to the Horse?: Popular sitcom. A innocent woodland becomes a legendary site of ghostly activity thanks to an inventive rumour spread by Alvin, Truly and Clegg.
Variations on a Theme of Road Rage: Roy Clarke's long-running sitcom about a group of sprightly seniors. When Howard buys a used car, it attracts the attention of a burly local.
New. Death by Vinyl: Final Chapter: Mattie investigates another murder involving the band when someone is stabbed with a knife, and it appears an incident from the past may be the key.
New. The Q Word: Mo McIntyre's grand plan to dig up an ancient stash of paramilitary weaponry gets under way, but he is unaware that he is already under surveillance and that MI5 are onto him.
James Bolam stars in the middle-aged detective drama. The team must identify a murder victim from just her torso, with the help of a bizarre pathologist.
